reviews.title: Two of four so defective I had to pull the plug to get my PC working after plugging them in.
reviews.text:
If I could give these negative five stars, I would. These are a four-pack of 64GB thumb drives that come in four colors. The first color, black, that I plugged into the PC hopelessly causes the PC to stall and become unresponsive to the point where I have to pull the power cord out to get the machine to turn off.

I tried the purple one after that, and it worked. Removed it okay. I tried the next one and hopelessly unresponsive again. That's two bad thumb drives in a four-pack. I didn't bother trying the last one as I don't particularly appreciate yanking the power out of my PC like that.

Maybe SamData is trying to sound like SanDisk? I don't know. I know that two out of four bad drives in a single package are statistically impossible unless you're just selling bad drives like an everyday business model.

Someone out to look into this as it seems a lot like fraud to me. These drives may well damage your computer since I had to pull the plug to get my machine back with two out of the four drives from a single package.
Worst thumb drives I have ever tried, bar none.
reviews.author: AGO6RBHKEFEJAAA4TUXYE7J47IKA

reviews.title: Works, but drags down my car's infotainment system
reviews.text: This unit does work, but it is too slow for me to recommend it to others. When writing music onto it from my Windows computer, the write speed was slow enough to repeatedly drop the data transfer rate all the way to zero. Other manufacturers' flash drives do not do that; they keep on going. The read speed is slow enough that it causes my car's infotainment system (2019 model year) to sometimes hang up. Indeed, at one point, it caused my backup camera to go to all black for a minute or two. Note that some other reviewers are apparently unware of what flash memory is like. It has a limited number of times that it can be written (rewritten), and it will wear out if you try to use it as a substitute for a SSD. It also isn't going to be nearly as fast as a SSD. Flash memory is most appropriate for putting mp3 files into your car's infotainment system and other applications that are write only a few times but read many times.

reviews.title: Great economy pack of excellent flash drives.
reviews.text:
My BIL recently asked me to restore a Microsoft Surface tablet that he purchased through eBay. It already had someone else's credentials and was locked. I spent hours, days, gathering the required components and instructional material to perform that task. (Note: Microsoft was a pretty decent source. But, I still had to modify their instructions somewhat to get the job done.) One thing I needed was a specific size flash drive. I didn't have one. So, I ordered these. I didn't need this many. But, I always have a use for them and they don't go bad. So, why not?

These MicroCenter flash drives are great. Using the latest (USB3.1) drivers, they connect instantly. They are already formatted to FAT32, the most common platform. They have a tiny red LED to indicate data activity. 64GB is plenty of storage for most of my uses. The speed at which they transfer data is fast enough for anything I could use them for. Good purchase.

reviews.title: Super Easy / Very affordable alternative to the subscription plan
reviews.text:
Had the system for a little over a year now, and a good 6 months were with the free cloud storage. I like the cloud storage, but I just don’t need another bill. At the time, the USB storage was not ready, but finally it came and for 14.99, it’s well worth the price! Click it in, get a notification, confirm the code and your recording clips again!

In testing the process, it records exactly as the cloud did, the clips are 100% viewable and deletable on the app. The only note, the clips load almost instantly on the cloud, but take maybe and extra second using the USB (who cares). All other functions (zones, clip length, sensitivity, etc…) are all present. The only features I noticed not available are the “snapshot” mode, which takes a photo each hour and plays them back, and the “Edit” mode for the cloud (where you can delete large groups of clips at once, instead of each one during view on the USB). Would I like these features, yes, do I consider it a deal breaker for paying the subscription fee? Absolutely not.

Great system for the price, and incredibly easy to use. The USB gives the recording back at a great price!

reviews.title: I'm returning it.
reviews.text:
2 Problems:
+ The file transfer speed is SLOOOOOW!
+ At least half of the files are corrupted.

I'm using this with a over-the-air DVR that records video in the very large MTS format to a USB drive. I then transfer the videos off the USB stick to my computer, convert them to MP4s, and transfer them back to the stick.

I was using a smaller 64GB Verbatim USB 2.0 drive. That was doing the job very well, but wanted more available storage.

Because I'm recording over the air broadcasts, it didn't occur to me right away that the files were possibly corrupted during the recording process until I realized that the the same number of similarly sized files that took 10 minutes to transfer off the old drive were taking HOURS on the new stick.

Plus, for the first time, I was seeing my transcoder throwing error after error, leaving the resulting MP4s jumpy and with audio that was no where close to being synced to the video!
reviews.author: AFZ45T266FQL4BHZPMQBZZJ7ZHEQ

reviews.title: Atrocious Write Speeds
reviews.text: Got a couple of these for free after promo points from a has been department store that's circling the drain.  The drives are cheaply constructed and light weight, but shouldn't break very easily unless you're rough on them.  I wouldn't recommend leaving them in your pocket for example, but in a USB holder or maybe a shirt pocket it should be fine.  The thing I don't like is that I'm getting a whopping 3 Mbs write speed on these.  To give you an idea, the podcasts I listen two now average about 30 MB, so it takes 10 seconds to transfer 1 segment.  Wow!  Could be I have a bad one, but it works otherwise so I think it's just cheap/slow memory used.  I also got the 8 GB version and that one is a much more respectable 8 Mbs transfer rate, which still isn't great, but is more livable.  I'd think the best use would be as a Windows install disk or something that doesn't need file updates very often.  Cheap so worked out well enough, but I have found much faster San Disk ones for cheaper on sale before.

reviews.title: Finding this device was truly a Godsend for me
reviews.text: My husband passed away in April and when I attempted to transfer photos and videos from his i-phone to my laptop, I could never get all of them to transfer. Even when it did attempt to transfer some of the photos, it was taking hours. I was worried about cancelling his phone service until I had everything transferred off. Considering that I have lost his income and only have mine now, that $60+ a month makes a difference. Finally I found a blog where someone suggested this flash drive so I checked it out and I am so thankful I did! I plugged the device into his phone and it prompted me to download the app, which took less than a minute. Then I was prompted to select the files I wanted to transfer. I went to his photos/videos, selected ALL, and I was amazed how fast they downloaded! I don't think it even took more than 10 minutes! I was so relieved! I then plugged the device into my laptop and easily downloaded all the photos and videos in just minutes. And I could finally have his phone service removed from our plan. This device also has a C type plug for Android phones that slides onto the USB end of the device. Super easy to use either way. I would highly recommend it to anyone.
reviews.author: AEOHQGWNBPJUTXZX7BKPZAA35CMA

reviews.title: Fast and easy to use. 57.9 GB free space
reviews.text: This Flash Drive is USB 3.0, so data transfers quickly. It holds 64GB and comes with 57.9 GB of free space. There is a shield that rotates around the outside of the flash drive. You rotate it away from the USB connector when you want to use the flash drive and rotate the shield over the connector to protect it during storage. There is a very small D-ring at the end of the shield so you can hang the flash drive off of something, though it's hardly wide enough even for piece of string. It was plug-and-play and does what I expected it to, so I'm happy with it.

reviews.title: Works perfect!
reviews.text: Very happy with this flash drive!  As an artist who self-publishes books, I want to be able to save my book files externally as a backup.  Each of my book files is anywhere from 1GB to 3GB.  I was able to save all my files for 15 books on this drive and there is room for at least 10 more.  Sweet!  The files transferred faster than I anticipated and my computer is older.  They also open quickly when I need to access them with the flash drive.  I also like that there is no special software to install, so the drive was ready to go.  Love this little flash drive, highly recommended!

reviews.title: Works as Expected

I figure that when I'm gone, it will be easier for my family to access old pictures on the flash drives, then trying to log into my cloud account.  I've been slowly copying all of our photos on to flash drives and then storing them in a fire proof box. This worked as expected. For the price, it's a lot of storage. No complaints from me.

reviews.title: Good utility storage, average speed, good value
reviews.text:
This is a useful card for what I want to do with it: specifically, store video and game files for a car entertainment system (a great way to keep kids entertained on long road trips!). Our old 32GB stick was running out of space, so I'm more than happy to test out this 64GB in it's place!
So far so good- it is able to store media and play it back without any hiccups. It definitely lives up to the USB3 speed in read- see the attached benchmark showing 111+MB/s, well over the USB2.0 average of ~40-60MB/s. However the write speed leaves much to be desired, averaging around 23MB/s even over USB3.
That's perfectly fine for use like ours- mainly, write once but read many times. If you want to use this to copy a lot of data over to it quickly, you might want to buy storage with faster write times. But for the price, this is a good value.

reviews.title: Good price, functionality and features are as described
reviews.text:
I had no problem using this device or downloading the related app. I did a few test copies to and from the device and my laptop and had no issues with it or the adapter. The accompanying device directions could be clearer. The only other issue I have is I can't plug the device into my iPod Touch with the case I have - it's too thick for the connector to plug in. This means I have to remove the case in order to use the drive. I'm thinking about cutting out the bottom of the case to resolve this.

EDIT:
The drive doesn't function properly. I copied 400mb of mp3 music to it. None of the songs would play using the drive's internal player or a 3rd party player. Included in the music were 2 folders with additional mp3 files. The folders copied over, then after the transfer was complete, there was nothing in the folders.

The password protection is also incredibly easy to crack because it's not tied to the device, but to the app. Just delete the app, then reinstall it and you have access to everything on the drive.

You get what you pay for. Buyer beware.

reviews.title: Decent price for name brand, but USB2 speed
reviews.text:
I love and trust Lexar products for my work jobs (professional grade memory cards and other solid state storage), so I had high expectations for this product. I love the build quality of this material, it's a solid feeling metallic body instead of cheap plastic, and it gives the impression of a product that will hold up to the test of time.
The price is also impressive for a name brand product with this kind of build quality. However, I had almost forgotten how slow USB2.0 can be. I'm so used to USB3 storage speeds of easily over 150MB/s, that the benchmarks on this one seem almost painfully slow at around the 20MB/s mark. But, that's par for the course with the older standard, so I can't really fault the product for it. This is a great value but you could easily get a much faster storage stick for a bit more money. I could see this being useful for someone who wants to distribute a hard-copy of something by hand and would like to order a bulk supply of durable and fast-enough but still affordable media. Otherwise, I think I'd recommend one that is USB 3 compatible at least since you can always be backwards compatible with 2.0 if necessary, but have the increased speed when available.
reviews.author: AGDSEYGSA5K664EUHWKV3ARDXO2Q

reviews.title: Works great, handy loop, capacity marking would be helpful
reviews.text:
These are great.
They work easily and are recognized (so far) by all devices that use them.
The handy built in loop makes it easy to attach a ring or a tether to make them easier to have at hand. The price for this 10 pack makes it affordable enough to use and give away without hesitation.
As with other similarly designed thumb drives, the silver swivel cover is just held onto the drive by resistance. That silver metal is very soft and pliable, which means it can easily be pulled or slip off the drive if it gets snagged on something in your bag or desk drawer.
That's easily remedied though, by just squeezing it together to create enough "spring" and tension to hold it in place when you slip it back onto the knobs again.
The only thing I would wish, is that there were some sort of marking on the drive itself that designated the storage capacity.
I have purchased other devices from this same company in other capacities, and there is no way to tell them apart. Of course, you can always run it to find out, but it would be easier to just know which one to pull out of the drawer.
I've taken to using a label maker to mark all of them so I know which one to pick up. A little tedious to do, but very helpful indeed.

reviews.text:
I bought the 128 GB drive and its storage is great. Good read / write speed on a 3.0 USB. I tested 118 MB/s Read and 72 MB/s Write speeds using Windows 10 tester.
The biggest issue is the drive isn't shielded and if plugged in next to a mouse or keyboard it will interrupt the signal causing your mouse to move sporadically and typing to freeze for a second or 2. Its very annoying so you should only plug in the drive when you need it and remove it when your finished.

I should have heeded the warnings from the others here about these drives, but a deal that sounded good had to be taken, I lost all of my wife's files due to this transferring to the drive and it just stopped working and when I tried to check what files I had, all I saw were empty folders, then I tried to start over and NOTHING, it stopped working and couldn't even format it.
Be smart stay away from these, hell just to transfer a gig at first took almost 30 mins or longer.
they call them FLASH DRIVES because they transfer in a FLASH, not like a old floppy disk, sheesh I remember 3.5 floppies being faster than this.
Gonna stick with Sandisk for now on.

reviews.title: If you need speed, keep looking
reviews.text:
Expecting 80MB reads and 30MB writes I got 15.2MB reads and 10M on a USB 3.1 using Crystal Disk Mark
I thought perhaps it was my computer, tested another USB in the same port, and was rocking 80/95... ok, not a port limitation... perhaps it's just not happy with my windows box so I plugged it into a USB-C and an android that regularly pushed 100+MB/s

Screenshot from Android attached - writes topping out at 9.27MB/s and reads at 13.88 - so slightly slower than what I got on the Windows unit is what I got on the Android unit... I'd say it's fair to say Windows and Android are reporting the same thing, and the only commonality is this USB drive.

On the windows box I decided to open device manager, optimize for speed, you know what you do. Noted that it did not have a write caching option, but the optimize for speed made no difference. Returning it.

reviews.title: Great way to swap file with friends and very inexpensive USB Pendrive. All ten were preformatted and worked with no problem.
reviews.text:
I wanted these to give to friends (as needed). So was looking for something cheap. Many laptops no longer have CD/DV so I opted for a low storage value USB 2.- flash drive. I finally settled on the slightly more pricey 1GB drives for 'future' larger files. I'm running Windows 10 Pro on an ASUS laptop with USB 3.0. (No problem) I spot checked several using Defender and Malwarebytes (Premium) and all were ok.They were FAT 32 preformatted with 958 MB available. I purchased the RED units so I could easily find them.
I did do a slight mod" I put a number with a black felt marker on the Red bump that the metal protector rotates on. That way I know if I see the number the orientation is right. You don't need to do any of that. These seem inexpensive as hard to find low value SD cards and not everyone has a SD reader. When you plug them in (these) glow bright red and flash with data transfer....The Silver metal protector rotates and can be easily popped off if you don't want it The lower storage values are Very inexpensive. Can even fit on a key ring...
The Size in my case 1GB is marked on the bag but NOT the device.
Company I purchased from was Fbsctech that sells other related computer devices. (always check the storefront w/3rd party suppliers)
Very happy and would purchase again from Fbsctech...

reviews.title: Ten out of Ten Good
reviews.text:
After reviewing the dozens of offerings, I chose these flash drives to distribute my 100-page paper at a conference. The paper takes up less than 1% of the drive's small capacity (which is MB not GB). I inserted each drive directly into the Windows-10 computer (not the USB hub) and slid the PDF file across to the USB drive in the file directory. Then I opened and closed the file on the drive to verify the copy. I used the "eject" function before removing the drive. Ten out of ten flash drives worked -- no failures. I went back and tested a random sample of two and the document was good. At a price of just under $2 per drive, this is not the cheapest you can buy, but since 100% of them work that is the actual price. I will order another batch of ten for the conference. Everybody likes a freebie and a flash drive is useful and who knows, somebody might even read my paper.

reviews.title: Mostly good, a few issues
reviews.text:
I purchased a 10-pack of these drives, and they're fine, except for a few issues:
• The LEDs that indicate activity as pictured in one of the product photos (inside the circle made by the cover pivot) aren't visible in the darker colors (black and dark blue) so there's only a tiny LED on the end, which cannot be seen easily when it's sticking out of the side of a laptop.
• I tested all 10 with a utility that tests them thoroughly, and they all passed without a problem, except one that failed hard (and I can't even see it in Disk Management). Amazon Support was good enough to issue a credit for the bad one.

reviews.title: 1 Failure and the other very slow
reviews.text:
I was trying to replace a few older 2.0 SanDisk flash drives with something a little newer. These caught my eye and I decided to give them a try. After testing their speed using the standard software, I noticed something weird. They have a burst of speed at the start but die out quick. I tried another one and same result. I was trying to set one up as a Ubuntu usb but the speed of the drive made it unusable. It was at least 10x slower to get into th OS then the 5 year old SanDisk Cruzer I use. I reached out to the company amd they said to basically send them back to Amazon through the normal return channel. I'm always extremely pleased with the ease of returns through Amazon and that was the only high point of the whole purchase.

When I formatted the drives to send back, one threw itself into read only mode and caused a BSOD. I cant possibly recommend this product to anyone, and I would stick with the brands most people are familiar with.

reviews.title: Your First Defense Against Windows Boot Drives
reviews.text:
We had a very tough lesson in jump drive space recently when my son's computer ran out of space, the Windows update failed, and then it got stuck in a Blue Screen of Death loop in which it kept trying to fix the issue but didn't have enough space to do so. To fix the problem, we needed a Windows Boot Drive, but had no jump drive large enough to accommodate the size needed. Stupid me, I thought I'd use my son's external hard drive instead.

What I didn't know, and what Windows didn't sufficiently warn me about, is that it would create an entirely new partition on the drive for the Windows Boot instead. We fixed the issue with my son's computer but had a new issue, as 1.4 TB of space was now in an inaccessible partition. We had to use special software to restore it.

End to end, it took for days to fix this problem, and it would all could have been avoided by just having a large enough jump drive to install Windows Boot. So that's why my son is getting this drive as a Christmas gift.

reviews.title: Tight fit
reviews.text: This is a 64GB Flash Drive USB 3.0. The drive is physically a tight fit in the USB slot. I have to push hard to get it fully connected and then I've noticed that it's scrapping the metal case of the drive because it's a tight fit. As for speed, the first time I tried to load about 3 GB of MP4 video and assorted .pdf files. The file were super fast, but the MP4 files took about 20 minutes. I was surprised, so I later added a 3 GB video and then 2 videos that totaled 3 GB. Both took just under 4 minutes, which is more what I'd expect. So the speed is fine now, though still not super-fast. Not sure what the original slowness was about.

reviews.title: Works well, good value, very generic, LED is hard to see
reviews.text:
I've bought these twice, a few months apart. Each time, I tested every one, by mounting them and writing a file, and they all worked.

The first time there was no logo on the metal shield, and they were formatted with a MS partition table and one VFAT partition. They show up in lsusb on Linux as "058f:6387 Alcor Micro Corp. Flash Drive". I've used them for lots of things now, and have had zero problems.

The second batch had the VICFuN logo on one side. They were formated without a partition table. The entire drive was one VFAT file system. They show up in lsusb as "abcd:1234 Unknown". That's the most generic usb id I've ever seen. I haven't used them for anything yet, but every one mounted and let me write a file.

On both batches, the activity LED is hard to see. A friend has the white version of one of these, and the LED shows clearly through the plastic body. These dark blue ones don't pass the light nearly as well. You can still see the LED dimly in the circular groove where the metal shield turns.

Overall, these are a good value.

Update, 2018.2.17, I've bought a third 10-pack. They work just as well as the first two batches. They're kind of like a combination of the first two batches. They don't have a logo on the cover, but they do have a small VICFuN logo on the usb connector. There's nothing to show what capacity they are, but they show up as the correct size when mounted. They have a MS partition table, with one vfat formated partition. They have the "abcd:1234 Unknown" usb id.

reviews.title: Good deal on nice little sticks!
reviews.text:
Flash drives are sure getting cheap...as in price I mean. I do photography work and like to deliver my work to clients on flash drives. Getting these 8 GB drives for roughly $5 a piece is a steal. I've only used a couple of them so far but I can recommend them. They are immediately recognized by my computer and the write speed for these is very good esp for USB 2 sticks. Add to that, people seem to like the little added "gift" of getting their pics on a free, pretty decent quality 8GB drive (maybe they don't realize how inexpensive these drives have become). Why get 8GB drives when I could get 2GB or 4GB for a little less? When you give these away you're actually giving people something they can use for a long time to come.

So, grab a few and give to clients or friends in the office or use them for stocking stuffers this Christmas.

reviews.title: Started with 10, 4 work now.
reviews.text:
From random write errors to straight up failures, it was a bad batch for me. Most worked for a while. One failed on a Mac that used it fairly heavily, one failed connected to an Aukey hub (will disconnect in the middle of a write operation,) another failed plugged into a Server 2019 Dell server of some sort (probably from 2016,) and it lasted about 4 months with relatively minor use.

Got one that exists - I can see it exists, but nothing on it - then I format/partition/it's there, unplug and plug in elsewhere and no partition. Like all writes are transitory.

So I believe I purchased these in 2018, we don't trust them for anything other than when I have to move something to a laptop

reviews.text: I bought this USB drive to free up space on my phone and to save pictures and videos. I'd read some reviews that were positive, but many that were negative. I decided to give it a try anyway to form my own opinion. First off, I have a Samsung galaxy S 20. In order to even use this product, I needed to remove my phone case. Fine. I removed it and plugged in the drive to my phone. It fit. I didn't try to read the instructions, and it took me a little while to figure out how to transfer files. I figured it out on my own. In seeing the ominous negative reviews, I decided to copy my pictures to the drive rather than just transferring them. I'm glad I went with this decision. Copying the files wasn't a problem. I decided to then actually transfer a few videos instead of copying them to the drive. To test the negative reviews, I removed the device and then plugged it right back into my phone. Immediately, I got the message that my phone wasn't compatible with the drive and that it would have to be formatted to be used. I tried to remove the drive and reinsert it several times, just in case this was an error, but I continued to receive the same message. If I formatted it, I would lose any files I have on the drive. I will say that I have yet to attempt to connect the drive to a computer to see if the files can be accessed this way. I will say that there is still plenty of space left on the drive, but this does not matter if I would have to erase all prior saved files JUST to transfer/copy more files. If this drive is already giving me this much trouble with my phone, I can only imagine that it won't be much different with my laptop. What is the purpose of this drive if saved files cannot be accessed? Why waste time and money if the product does not work as advertised?

reviews.title: Subscription appears to be the only way to make it work
reviews.text: I hoped I wouldn’t have to write this review but the total failure of the Blink system compels me to. When I first installed the Blink doorbell, extra camera & sync unit the 30 day free subscription to their cloud was activated. During that time the system appeared to work just fine. They stated that you have the option to subscribe to their cloud for $100/$120 per year for all devices or use local storage (USB) on the sync system. I opted for the local storage. The day the trial was over the system stopped working. I would get the occasional notification through Alexa but no videos whatsoever. I called tech support and they were helpful by having me reset the system – simply unplug, wait 30 sec and plug back in. That seemed to work. However, the next day same problem and the same solution was applied. However, I have to go through that procedure every day now. Plus, the extra camera has stopped working altogether. Conclusion: the local storage option is totally bogus. It only works if you reset the system daily which rules out any security if you’re away from home. 60+ days since purchasing rules out a refund so I am going to put it in a box and search for a different brand of video security.

reviews.title: Wow! Easy & Fast.
reviews.text:
I got this so I could back up my 2019 16" MBP to upgrade the OS. All I can say is "WOW"! It copied everything so quickly.

I'd bought another one --the brand we all think of first for a thumb drive-it wouldn't work.
It wouldn't copy anything. I wasted hours trying and trying to figure out why it wasn't working, then more time on their website trying to figure out if I had to do something, like format it or ??, so it would copy. Never found anything. I wrote to them --they never responded. Gave them a few weeks
because of the pandemic and thinking maybe they had a lot of people out..but nothing. Finally, I gave up and will never buy one of their products again.

Wasting so much time while more & more things wouldn't work because I needed to upgrade my OS.
I got so frustrated. Finally, I came looking for another thumb drive to back-up with. I spent hours researching here, on Amazon, reading about them, reading reviews, etc. and settled on this one:
THKAILAR USB Flash Drive- 128GB USB Thumb-2-in-1 Memory C Stick.... .

It arrived quickly but I was somewhat afraid to try it because of my other experience— I stalled.
Finally this morning I got some courage. I plugged it in and dragged a file onto it. Copied! Dragged another file. Copied! I opened a few to make sure. Everything was as it should be. This is what
I had expected with the first one. So, this morning, I copied everything that isn't part of the OS onto this drive in short order. I'm so impressed at how quickly it copied everything.

If you need to back up your data, and have a Mac that meets the compatibility requirements, buy this drive. As for other functions, or other devices, I don't know. I bought it for a specific purpose and it did what I wanted done quickly and easily. I suspect it will for everything they say you can do on appropriate
equipment.

reviews.text: This was pricey compared to a lot of "similar" products, but I bought it because of the reviews saying it was easy to use (compared to other products that many reviews said were difficult to figure out). Used it to download about 1200 photos from iPhone and BONUS it let us download the phone contacts too. You do have to have decent wifi service, but if you have that and you can download the app and hit start, the app does the rest.  Once downloaded onto the flash drive, I stuck the drive in my MacBook and quickly copied all the photos onto the computer. I was slightly confused if I was going to be able to do that, but it worked just like I wanted. Yes you can buy something cheaper, but for the time, frustration and making sure you have all your memories, this is the way to go.

reviews.title: Well Made and Very Useful
reviews.text:
I just bought an iPad to use with Procreate. I write picture books and am taking classes to learn to illustrate them (I'm also an artist.) I have scanned sketches on my PC and need to transfer them to the tablet. These thumb drives do the trick. I can save my files to the thumb drives, then transfer them to other devices easily.

Constructed with silvery mat-finished metal, they are sturdy and handsome. There's a loop at the top of each so you can add a tag or attach them to a key chain.

They would make a nice addition to a gifted iPad.

reviews.title: Needs a Little Improvement as a Key Chain.
reviews.text:

I keep my key chain in my pocket, along with lint and other junk. As you can tell from the photo, one connector will always be exposed. So it would be nice if this could be designed so the connectors could be covered and protected. Then it could be 5 stars.

reviews.title: Almost Perfect
reviews.text:
Like so many people, I have quite a collection of thumb drives. One fine day after hunting through various places to find that one I needed, I decided the time had come to get an organiser that would hold them all, or at least most of them.
One of the things I like best about this is that it holds both styles. There are elastics for the older, longer ones and nets for the new, short thin ones. You can fit two of them in those.
I’m not crazy about the two sided bit. I almost invariably unzip the one I don’t need.
The other not perfect thing is that I have a couple I use for lots of things. I usually keep them on lanyards days I need them. Filled, it’s overstuffed with the lanyards. I’ll probably just store them elsewhere.
Even so, it’s close enough that I will survive it. With the lanyards in one container and the rest in another I still know where everything is.
So, happiness is possible.
I hope this is helpful to someone.

reviews.title: Fast as they claim!
reviews.text:
Fast, fast, fast! See my Crystal Disk Mark shots. First was formatted NTFS. Next is in exFAT. Not too big of difference. I threw in my SK hynix Gold S31 500GB SATA Gen3 2.5 inch Internal SSD that is rated up to 560MB/S. Lastly, I added for LOL, my 500GB Samsung 970 EVO Plus NVMe M.2 SSD. All three drives were on the same computer while benchmarking. The Vansuny USB 3.1 Flash Drive will copy large files at 350+ MB/s without slowing down. I tried a couple read and write transfers up to nearly 30 GB without any slowing down due to running out of a tiny high speed drive cache like a lot of other manufacturers drives do. This Vansuny drive acts more like SATA 3, 600MB/s SSD desktop drives rather than a USB flash/thump drive. You may find another drive as fast in sequential transfers, but they will lack in the random read/write speed like in the real world. File systems are random, not perfect sequential transfers for showing off fast benchmarking numbers. I noticed they have a 500GB version available again. I would have grabbed it if available at the time of my purchase.

The one con is the drive does not have a premium feel to it. Almost cheap in feel despite being made from aluminum alloys. I care about speed first, then price and lastly does it feel like Apple made a flash "i Drive" !!!

One last thing, go into Windows Device Manager and put the drive into performance mode, not fast disconnect mode that is on by default when new USB drives are first plugged into Windows. If I read one more review slamming a drive for being slow and knowing they are clueless to what they write, I will scream! LOL

reviews.title: Nice price, decent speed dual end for easy transfer!
reviews.text:
This is a nice USB-C and USB-A drive. Physically, it's a little larger than some. It's got a nice area at the top where you could put a keyring through. It is a sleek looking matte silver color and does not have a cap to cover the exposed end. It swivels in the center so you can use either the USB-C end or the standard USB-A end. When it swivels, it makes a satisfying click at the halfway point (when it makes a T shape) and as the end snaps into place. My computer says that when only the necessary to work programs are on this drive it has a functional 58.5G free. The drive does start to feel hot after being attached to my laptop for a while.

My phone will use a USB-C drive. At first, I thought this didn't work because my phone couldn't see it. But when I plugged it into the computer, Windows wanted to "repair" it as it hadn't been formatted or anything first. After that, it works fine on my phone, laptop, desktop, and PS5. That's all I've tried it on so far! I was able to transfer pictures, audio clips, pdfs and video from one device to another.

This tests as having a 152.4Mbps read speed and a write speed of 46Mbps with SpeedOut. Testing with CrystalDiskMark, I got an average of 145Mbps read and 44Mbps write over several tests. This is also what the listing says the speed is. A USB 3.0 flash drive should have a read speed of 60-150Mbps and a write speed of 10-45Mbps.

reviews.title: Sleek design but very...very slow write speeds.
reviews.text:
This SSK USB could be convenient, if used with several platforms (Android, MacOS & Windows) and use mostly to read files.
The write speed is as fast as a USB 2.0 standard.

The posted 150 MB/s transfer speed, could be referring to the read speed and it was able to reach 152MB/s.

But, there is also a posted CrystalDiskMark test image showing write speeds of 123.49 MB/s over USB C 3.1.
I ran several versions on CrystalDiskMark over USB C 3.2 Gen2 and I could not get write speeds above 35MB/s.
I could not even get above 23MB/s average while writing data to the SSK USB flash drive over a faster USB C 3.2 Gen2 standard on several PCs.
The SSK achieved the fastest write speed on a MacBook Pro while transferring a 4.3GB video file. It took 2 minutes 42 seconds reaching an average of 26.5MB/s write speed.
Those speed are the top speed when the SSK USB flash drive was half empty.
When the drive was above 75% capacity, it could not even keep USB 2.0 speeds...taking 27 minutes 11 seconds to write the same 4.3GB video file 2.6 MB/s

If you want a convenient, sleek looking dual interface USB A / USB C USB flash drive that you could connected to several devices, then the SSK flash drive will serve you just fine.
But, if you are looking for USB 3.0 write speeds, look somewhere else.

By the way, I have a 128GB USB flash drives that cost the same as this SSK 64GB unit and the write speed is twice as fast.

Some think of it as an iPhone Flash Drive, iPhone Storage, or iPhone Memory expansion.Apple MFi Certified - Guaranteed to work with all Apple devices with iOS 9.0+ and Lightning Connector including iPhone X / 8 / 8 Plus / 7 / 7 Plus / 6s / 6s Plus / 6 / 6 Plus / 5s / 5c / 5 / iPad Pro / iPad Air / iPad mini / iPod touchEasy to use - Simply plug it in, and download the Bolt app from the App Store**, and then back up your camera roll. The Bolt will make storing your iPhone or iPad photos and videos stress-free. With the Bolt you can take photos and videos directly to the device instead of using your iPhone or iPad storage. The camera feature can record video in 720p, 1080p, or 4K. Pictures are saved directly to the device when plugged in and the app is used. The camera feature of the Bolt app is so similar to the existing iPhone and iPad you won’t feel the need to learn how to use a whole new feature.Secure – The Kingston Bolt also features an app security feature. This allows a user to set a password or touch ID in order to access the app with their Bolt, protecting against unauthorized use on their iPhone or iPad.Portable – The Bolt makes extra storage for iPhone or iPad accessible at any time with the included key ring accessory. Many people find that iPhone and iPad storage are difficult to transport. The key ring accessory that is included with the Kingston Bolt can easily be attached to your car keys so it is always with you. Sleek Design – The Bolt is a very small form factor unlike many of our competitors. You would be hard pressed to find a similar iPhone Flash or USB storage device that is small

reviews.title: EASY TO USE!!!
reviews.text: Works great, and is refreshingly easy to use! I plugged it into my iPhone, was prompted by my iPhone to install the app, (and it took me right to the app in the App Store). Once the app was installed, it was one easy step to copy all my photos onto the Kingston Bolt. It gives you the option to copy them on the Bolt only; copy them to Bolt & delete from iPhone; or to save the photos you're taking to the Bolt, instead of on your phone. You simply plug the other end into any USB drive to then transfer the photos to your computer or laptop. I first ordered a "G-Ting Jump Drive" for copying my photos from my phone to my computer, but after 10 tries (at least!) I couldn't figure it out. Kept getting error messages, and what few photos it finally copied wouldn't even show up on my computer. Piece of junk -- I'm usually very good with technology.

reviews.title: Easy to go from phone to computer
reviews.text:
I wanted a quick way to transfer files from my Z Flip 3 to my Surface Pro. I know you can do it wirelessly but sometimes old school with a usb stick is just easier.

Physical attributes:
1. USB 3.0 on one side and USB-C on the other
2. Caps for each connector and a lanyard that connects to main body of stick

3.0 speeds are decent. Pic shows around 60mb + but that was connected through a usb-c hub. When I connected straight to a usb 3.0 slot it was more like 150mb +

I may get some more of these for separating picture/video storage. Once I figured out how to transfer files from android's file system. It was really easy to just plug and play. I used my phone to format the drive and away I went.

Overall I like the design of this USB stick. We will see how long the caps and everything last...but so far things are looking up and I don't expect any issues.

reviews.title: Works even better than I expected!
reviews.text: I was looking for a USB drive to add storage space to install apps on my First TV Cube version 2.  I needed it to be quick enough to save files and run applications off of for my Fire TV Cube.  I found this drive and gave it a try since it was so cheap for a 64GB USB 3.x thumb drive. It cost me $8 and some change.  Their website states the drive as "32GB-64GB: Read speed up to 100MB/s".  I am getting well beyond what they state.  CrystalDiskMark tested it at 170MB/s reads and 91MB/s writes!  I found this to be true with all TEAMGROUP drives (USB, NVME, SATA SSD).  They under promise and then deliver you much more.  I like that as it is a welcome change from most companies out there.  Most companies fail to even come close to what their claims are.  I added my super-fast Vansuny USB 3.1 Flash Drive 256GB to compare this cheap 8 buck drive with.  The Vansuny is rated at 400MB/s Super Speed Flash Drive, USB 3.1 Gen 2.  If you need a cheap drive that is also fast, look no further than TEAMGROUP's Model C162 USB drives!

reviews.title: Cost effective flash drives
reviews.text:
These are very useful USB2.0 flash drives. Transferring about 20 hours of 1080p video lectures in files totaling almost 14Gb took just under 20 minutes. This needed the use of a small adapter from a USB-C port to accommodate the USB-A drive, but most computers still have USB-A ports. Writing was from a fast SSD drive, using a late model iMac with an A1 processor. Clearly the limiting factor was the flash drive, but the speed was completely acceptable. Once the files were on the drive, there was no problem playing them.

The drive swivels into an aluminum sleeve which protects the contacts. There is very little weight, and the aluminum sleeve has a small hook that would enable the drive to be attached to a keyring or lanyard. I would be reluctant to put one of these in a pocket, where lint could enter the minimally protected connector. This was the only weakness.

These drives have operated well, and coming in a pack of 5 for at low cost makes them a good bargain.

reviews.title: Where are  my files

The file played perfectly fine on my phone UNTIL I backed it up to the Flash drive. I have over 500 photos/recordings that are now useless and gone forever, because I trusted this piece of crap.  The whole point of a flash drive is so we can trust to keep our data safe.  Clearly this device is failing.

reviews.title: Not Fast Enough Read Speed for Dashcam
reviews.text:
This was supposed to be fast enough for our Tesla sentry and dash cameras but we kept getting alerts that some clips were not saved due to the slow write speed. We purchased a different product, a SanDisk High Endurance micro SDXC card with an adapter and it works flawlessly. I do not recommend SanDisk 64GB Ultra Fit USB 3.1 Low-Profile Flash Drive for dash and sentry cameras.
Get this instead: https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B084J46RH4/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_title_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&th=1

reviews.title: Comparable to the more expensive name brand usb drives,reached advertised write/read speeds
reviews.text:
Update:
After four months with this drive on my keychain, enduring many drops and other daily damage, this drive continues to perform as it did when I first received it. I still use it weekly to transfer files from my macbook to my android phone without any issues. When using it on a windows laptop or desktop though I routinely get a "this drive must be scanned for errors" message but I've yet to lose any data. The drive still gets hot with larger file transfers but cools down quickly.

Perfect usb-c/usb a thumb drive to place on your keychain and forget about until you need to make a quick file transfer to either your phone or your laptop. My Samsung s8 instantly detected the external drive over usb-c connection and transfer speeds were fairly quick, definitely in the middle pack of usb3.0 thumb drives.

Attached a picture of a quick transfer test (drive already had 25gb on it).

Feels durable and I've had it on my keychain now for over a month with no issues. Use it to transfer voice recordings from my cellphone to a laptop for backup. Doesn't get hot during large transfers either so they've done a decent job with the cooling/heatsink.

I recommend this drive as it punches above its weight.

reviews.title: WHO knew you needed this?
reviews.text: I have a new cell phone and it is a Type C, so I really hadn't worried too much about transferring data, as I could just use a usb/C cord. BUT what about when you may not have a cord with you? AH. there lies the issue. And where this flash drive comes in VERY handy! I love being able to load it onto my phone, transfer files I worked on on it, then load them into my laptop or desktop and keep working on the SAME file, and not have to worry about a being connected to internet to download, And I can resave the updated version, flip the drive around and upload back into my phone. LOVE IT! I love how the app installed lets me know how long it will take to transfer files, so there's no guessing! My only minor quibbles are that I have to take my phone case partially off to use it, as I have a heavier rubber one on my phone (I am clutz, I admit it) and that there is no way to hang it on a keyring or attach to hook on briefcase, so it's subject to falling to the nether regions of any carrying case. but over all those only take away a 1/2 star.. Love this and you will too!
reviews.author: AEQ6EXUNL2KK635VOZUFUVUAVBLQ

reviews.title: Oh, Finally!
reviews.text:
I was talked into getting a Google Pixel when they first came out. Let's say I was less than enchanted with it, and I still refer to it as "U.P." (Useless Phone). One of the things I missed from my previous phone, a Droid Turbo, was the ability to use a feature called USB on the Go. This meant you could plug a thumb drive with the proper connection into the charging port and you could therefore copy files from the thumb drive to the phone and vice versa. I was astonished that you could not also do this with the fancy, much vaunted upon Pixel.

Over the years I have bought several of these double connector thumb drives (with a regulation USB connector at one end and a USB-C type connector at the other) in the vain hope that the newest model would connect with this daft Pixel, but no dice. Still hoping, I ordered this Leizhan thumb drive, which, once again, worked perfectly with my computer, but the stupid Pixel would not read the drive, even though it now had a different, upgraded version of the operating system than when I bought it.

And then...I asked for help again on the Pixel assistance board, and someone advised me what to do (it involved going into a setting menu and changing something), and this lovely flash drive FINALLY WORKS with my phone. I can't believe how long it took to get this to happen.

The drive is great; very fast, plugs securely into both the computer and into the phone. I am very pleased!

reviews.title: Can't receive shortwave
reviews.text: I've been in network and internet Radio since the mid-70's and know that shortwave isn't what it used to be.  However, there are still some countries that broadcast on shortwave and beam their signals toward The USA.  I was hoping to hear those signals, but this Prunus L-238 can't pick up diddly-squat when it comes to shortwave.  I live on The West Coast just 90 miles inland from The Pacific Ocean and radio signals are relatively easy to pick up here.  I went inside, I went outside, I went to a flat field, I used an external antenna - no shortwave no matter what time of day or night I tried.  The AM and FM signals are great - good and strong, but this little radio doesn't "do" shortwave.

reviews.title: 10-pack 8GB USB 2.0 stick.  A complete scam.
reviews.text: This was a ten pack.  I tested 5 out of the 10.  They all came preformatted with 3 partitions of "unknown" type, and one of type "Novell Netware".  Remember those?  From WAY back?  That was the first clue.  That several of the partitions were listed as being 100s of GB in size (the sticks are supposed to be 8GB) was the second clue.  I reformatted them with 1 DOS partition, and wrote 0's to the partition.  Three out of 5 failed with "end of media" errors at roughly 80% of the size of the partition.  Bad sign.  Two were able to write the whole partition, but when I tried to repeat the exercise a second time on one of them, it also failed with an end of media error before it could complete.  That was enough for me to return the item.  I did not attempt to write legitimate data and then read it back because there was already too many errors to bother with that test.
reviews.author: AENTLJQVO2AEHJJORPJWU4NNOS4A

reviews.title: inexpensive backup for usb 2 documents etc..
reviews.text: Ideal for school kids or for non-critical backups.  The read/write speeds are painfully slow if you are already used to USB 3 speeds  (for eg.  a name brand USB 3 drive for 32 GB provides closer to 200 MB/sec read speed, about 10X faster).  For small documents and presentations, this is OK, but if you plan to use it for streaming music, may not be optimal. reviews.title: Hilarious and Practical, But Not For Sensitive Data
reviews.text:
First off, this is clearly meant as a novelty item and I do not rely on it with sensitive or precious data the same way I do my more proven USB drives from name brand manufacturers.

That said, this is a seriously unique flash drive that looks every bit as cool in person as it does in the pictures!

It's rather large (probably larger than most would expect...see picture), but it still fits in most USB slot locations easily. The overmolded rubber design affords quite a bit of shock protection to the body of the drive and I get a kick every time I remove the large rubber "skull", which covers and protects the most sensitive end of the drive.

My wife got this for me at Christmas last year and I have used it regularly for non-sensitive data transfers and general day-to-day storage. It has held up well and has become one of my all-time favorite gifts!

The only reason I knocked off a star is because of the unknown reliability and the fact that transfer speeds seem to be consistently slower than most of my other drives.

If you like skeletons or want to give someone a unique gift that is practical enough to use and will garner a chuckle every time they do, this skeleton USB drive simply can't be beat!

Highly recommended!
reviews.author: AFGXMICVBMHATS3XS5QMMW7WZ4VQ

1 had the usb in backwards, no big deal flipped it back and the glue held.
1 was completely dead (I got 150x256MB so whatever)
1 had some weirdly named files and folders on it, but after it reformatted itself and then I reformatted a few times (make sure to leave it at 4096 kbytes and not click device defaults) it was working just fine so idk what that was about.
Outside of those 3 weird experiences, the rest of them seem to work just fine, I'm only adding ~210MB so not completely filling them but can verify it holds at least that.

I'm happy with the product I received at the price I got it for. Will buy again :)

reviews.title: Outer casing will scuff, overall good
reviews.text: I just received this flash drive so have not used it extensively yet. First, the packaging was easy to open. Unlike the usual packaging they had the plastic container secured by an adhesive dot so you only had to pop it open instead of the usually cutting and ripping apart the packaging. It's a bit bigger than my other 256GB flash drives. The outside is brushed metal but it appears to wear or scuff easily as the plastic holder was scuffed and the flash drive had scuffed through the packaging. Having said that, the metal housing does appear to sturdy. I was able to plug it into my phone (Samsung S9+) and it worked just fine. Having said that when I plugged it into my Windows laptop I got a message saying it needed to be fixed. Don't do it, just ignore the message and pick to use the drive anyway. The last time I let Windows fix a flash drive I could not use the USB C end of the flash. I could only use it with an adapter plugged into the USB 3.1 end. I transferred a few pics and it worked just fine. Speed was okay. So far so good. I will update if I have anything new to add.
reviews.author: AGAVKNCPQC4E4KVNDMKCNG74DLAA

reviews.title: Things you need to know
reviews.text:
When I first plugged this into my Windows 10 machine, everything on the entire machine started running in slow motion. I can see why some reviewers thought it contained a virus.

My best theory as to what was happening is that when a new device appears, Windows Defender wants to do a through scan to insure there are no threats. This takes many hours. Once I was able to leave it plugged in overnight, the problem went away.

The other problem was that I could only use half of the rated capacity. A Windows reformat using ExFAT cured that problem.

I tested the read and write speed and it was as expected.

The two plugs do not have dust protectors. I would not put this in your pocket without some additional protection. I plan on 3D printing caps.

reviews.title: Very Cute...One I Received Did Not Work
reviews.text:
I purchase this flash drive to load Disney photos for a family member. Paid extra for this cute Stitch drive but waste of time and money. One star for being cute but should be zero.

The first photo files started loaded fine but as I added more, files were corrupted or missing. After everything was loaded and I was checking the files, the drive went blank--said file location empty. Then and seconds later the drive didn't work at in any port on my computer. Checked other flashdrives and they all work so it is the drive....But then what can you expect with a drive that looks like Stitch?

Since we will need to be able to actually access the files, I will have to go with a name brand product rather than cute.

**UPDATE** The seller/Amazon did refund full purchase price and seller offered to exchange but I didn't want to test another one. Might be an anomaly.

reviews.author: AHR4BXCQRS2TDVZ3SGW3OCD6HBJQ

reviews.title: Great cheap battery replacement
reviews.text:
I have a GoPro 10. I also have that Volta handle which charges your gopro while you're using it. So normally, I'm not swapping out batteries. But, when I go swimming (volta is water resistant not water proof) or want to mount it to something, I need extra batteries. The standard GoPro batteries don't last that long. I admit, I don't time it, but anecdotally, I'd say I get about 1.5 hrs of straight recording (I was filming my friends band). Bottom line is, I need multiple batteries and I think that most gopro users will want extra batteries and a standalone charging case to boot. You might not use it all the time like me, but when you need it, it's a godsend. This is a basic battery charging case that comes with two batteries. There's a slot for 3 batteries, and I'm stating the obvious here, you can charge three batteries at once with a USB C cable. It includes one but in 2023 I imagine most people have a gang of them as micro USB is on its way out.

On the case there are lights that are either red or green. If the battery slot is empty or if it's charging, the light will be red. When it's charged, it goes green.

The batteries included have a slightly different shape, you can see my pictures; the official GoPro batteries are more square, whereas these have a rounded shake at the top. The aftermarket batteries fit snugly in the gopro, so there's no issue there.

The case itself is nice, it snaps shut so you need not worry about the lid opening on it's own and the batteries falling out. You have to physically flip it open. The batteries, while stored inside or otherwise charging, do not slide around. They too fit snugly.

The battery life is average. I admit I didn't time it, like the official GoPro batteries, but it seems as if they don't quite last as long. It's not a big deal when you get three in the chamber to swap out, but if you were stuck with just one after market battery it might not last as long as the "official" ones. But that's only a minor issue.

All in all, I'm extremely pleased with these batteries and more specifically the case. I already owned three batteries, so the extra two is just a bonus. Now I never have to worry about running out of power, no matter the filming situation I'm in. It's a great value.

reviews.title: Good Price & Fast Enough Speed
reviews.text: RAOYI 3 Pack 128GB Flash Drive Renewed 110MB/s USB 3.0 Flash Drive Colorful Thumb Drive Slide Retractable USB Drive Mini Portable Memory Stick with Lanyard Hole (Green, Red, Blue) is a better than average flash drive. I have always used SanDisk Drives for my main copies, but they are pricey, so I use less expensive drives for other secondary copies. One thing I like is when I first plug it in, there are no vault/codes I have to erase. Those take up space, so I always erase any attachments for security codes. The 128 GB flash drive actually has 117 GB of space on it. This is typical with all flash drives. As for downloading my photos and home movies on it, it went smoothly, pretty fast on my Windows 7. It works as well as my SanDisk, and time will tell how it holds the information. Having the plug retractable also is a huge plus for protection. For now, it’s a good price, fast enough and has nothing I have to erase with extra vault codes that take up space. 4 ½ STARS.

reviews.title: A thousand uses for these fantastic wrist lanyards!
reviews.text:
I only needed 2 of these, but who could resist this fantastic purchase of 50!
My purpose for needing these is so my mom could secure her car fob to her purse so she can easily find it when she needs it. For her car, the fob only needs to be in the car in order to start the car (there is no actual key to stick into the ignition), but she does need to actually have the fob in hand to lock and unlock the doors.
This lanyard allows the fob to be tethered to her purse strap and hang securely inside her purse. When she needs it, it's easy to find and reach by just fishing it out by the lanyard.
She gave a few to her sister who uses a cane. She attached this lanyard to the cane handle so even when she needs two hands for something, she can still have her cane at hand.
She shared half of her bounty with me, and I've already used some of them. I copied her and did the same with our car fobs. Our car automatically unlocks as we approach, but I still need the fob to lock it.
We will also be going on a snowboarding trip soon, and I will be putting these temporarily on our phones so there is no worry about dropping or losing our phones in the snow.
I even attached one of these to my wallet and turned it into a wristlet for the times I want to leave my purse behind and just grab my wallet to run a quick errand.
These are wrist lanyards, so understand they are not long enough to be worn around your neck. It's a bit of a struggle to slip a gloved hand through the strap (snowboard gloves are bulkier than normal dress gloves though). Bare hands for myself and husband are no problem.
These are light weight/light use quality, so keeping that in mind, they seem of high quality. I wouldn't be using them to hang anything of weight, but to use for convenience, they are fantastic.
50 of these sound like a lot, but I can see myself finding more and more uses for these as time goes by.
reviews.author: AGH4YIW3THVSFYUMVXMJC57W5G7A

reviews.title: Proceed with caution. Mediocre performance.
reviews.text:
So far, these drives work, but I would exercise caution in their use. I typically use flash drives of this caliber for one-time purposes, such as sending files to a client.

I tested these with Crystal Disk Mark, USB Flash Benchmark and CheckFlash, each of which is established as reliable.

Crystal Disk Mark reported “insufficient capacity” for testing. Not a good sign.

USB Flash Benchmark reported, with 512K blocks, 15.31MB/s Write and 34.21 MB/s Read. Not bad for an inexpensive USB 2.0 flash drive.

CheckFlash would test only with 4096B blocks (another red flag) and reported 9.11 MB/s Write and 32.52 MB/s Read. Kind of slow.

Overall, I would not entrust valuable data to these flash drives. I would, as I intend to do, use them for one-off situations, such as sending to other parties.

Jerry

reviews.title: Cute BUT OMG Sets New Record for SLOW!!!
reviews.text: I realize it's USB 2.0 but OMG this drive is painfully slow! Drive does marginally okay for small files under 1GB but anything over results in auto throttling to about 15mb/s. I think two things are wrong with this product. First, it's uses antiquated USB 2.0 technology. And secondly, I think the memory chip inside is a poor quality chip thus the painfully slow read and write. Drive might be okay for a young kid who has files under 1GB, otherwise stay away. The cuteness of the penguin is CERTAINLY not worth the price.

Some typical settings you may have to change in the BIOS to boot from the USB are disabling secure boot (sometimes called UEFI), enabling legacy mode and disabling fast startup. 4. reviews.title: Lifesaver!
reviews.text:
This usb really got me out of a jam! I have a dual boot PC with Win10/Ubuntu on it. Windows wouldn't boot so I ran their recovery utility from a usb. Windows never did recover and worse I picked an option the said it would just delete my files but not format the drive, but after that my dual boot menu was gone and Ubuntu wouldn't boot. So my PC was a brick. I downloaded a Ubuntu ISO on my macbook and copied to a usb key, but that usb wasn't bootable on the PC.

So rather than mess around I ordered this usb and the PC booted to it and I formatted the drive and made it all Ubuntu. bye-bye Windows! If you look at the cost and convenience it is a good value. Less than $10 difference between this and a blank usb key and you know this one works.

title: StarTech.com Standalone 1 to 2 USB Flash Drive Duplicator / Cloner / Eraser, Multiple USB Thumb Drive Copier / Sanitizer, System File / Sector-by-Sector Copy, 1.5 GB/min, 3-Pass Erase, LCD (USBDUP12)
description: This standalone USB duplicator lets you clone or erase two flash drives without connecting to a host computer. 1:2 Drive Duplication The USB cloner lets you copy up to two drives simultaneously. It offers asynchronous and synchronous duplication, with speeds of up to 1.5GB per minute. Asynchronous duplication lets you remove and replace a drive while the other drive continues to copy. The duplicator features two duplications modes: system and file or whole drive. System and file provides reduced duplication times. It copies partitions and stored data but ignores any sections with empty space. Whole drive takes more time because it does a sector-by-sector (mirror image) duplication of the drive. This duplication mode is ideal when you need to capture everything on the drive, including hidden data. Erase Two Drives Simultaneously The USB flash drive duplicator lets you wipe USB drives two at a time and supports several erase modes including: quick-erase, full-erase and secure-erase. Secure-erase meets global requirements, including the new NIST (SP 800-88 Rev1) standards, ensuring that all drives are properly wiped. Easy to Use The standalone USB duplicator features an LCD display so that you can monitor erasing and duplication to ensure completion. It also has four shortcut keys for easy operation. USBDUP12 is TAA compliant and backed by a StarTech. reviews.title: Great for transferring large files
reviews.text:
It seems like lately I need more devices instead of fewer ones. I try to centralize, but always seem to have so much information on so many different spaces. This really simplifies things. My phone is by far the most central device, but my computer and tablet do get use. When I need to send files that are just too large to share through bluetooth or to keep on a program they all share which they don't since they have different OS, this has come in handy. Considering I come from an era of DOS and floppy disks, a flash drive that is able to hold this much data still amazes me while at the same time I take for granted that something like this exists.

It's really small and easily fits in a pocket or can be worn on a necklace. It works on Mac and android. Amazing little drive.

It's a basic 16GB flash drive with a hard rubber coating. This is small in physical size though I've had an 8GB about that size for a long time. That one was fun because it had a rubbery exterior that made it bounce so I liked to call it the superball. This one won't bounce though.

reviews.author: AFEYHZ6ZZCDYHVCZKIEDM2HZTVSA

reviews.title: Uh, yeah, it's water resistant!
reviews.text:
I used this to transport documents and such, and when I did I left it in my pocket and it got washed in the washing machine. After it dried out, I tested it by copying a 10GB file onto it. And then accessed the file. I had no problem. It read just fine. I didn't test by running my car over it, but I don't think I'll be doing much where I need to concern myself with the crushing factor.

I did have to format it as NTFS, because when it came it was only FAT32. After I formatted it I was able to use the 14.5GB. It's just too bad this one wasn't a 3.0 USB drive. That would be even better.

I will likely purchase more of these for work since we do a lot of flash drive use when imaging our files. ToughMAX worked well for me.

reviews.title: Needs to be Formatted. Why? How?
reviews.text:
When I saw this flash drive, I knew I had to have it. The ability to transfer photos and files from computer to phone, back and forth, really appealed to me.

After plugging this into my iPhone, it immediately told me that I needed to go to the App Store and download a program called Y disk to transfer files.

Y disk is a terrible non-intuitive app that (at first) seems to only lets you copy 10 images at a time. With about 150 images for me to copy, it took me a while – until I discovered another way to copy a lot more images to the flash drive quickly.

Then I removed the flash drive and plugged it into my computer.

BIG PROBLEM.

I got a message telling me that I needed to format the drive before using it. And of course, I would lose all my data.

I started to quick-format it, but it would not allow quick formatting, so I used slow formatting. But that never worked. I tried with both exFAT and NTFS using many various allocation units, but nothing worked. In most cases it started to format. Some of the formatting options quit immediately, Most started for format for about 10 minutes before quitting. One lasted nearly an hour before it quit. But in all cases, it eventually gave ne the message that Windows can’t format it.

I understand this may not happen to everyone. Maybe it’s my computer itself that is having the problem formatting. But overall I now have this flash drive that is completely useless to me.

reviews.title: From one classy person to another
reviews.text:
My main goal in life is tricking people into thinking I'm a classy, sophisticated lady, when I am in fact a human trash fire. This thumb drive is definitely helping me step up my game. Would a garbage person own this? Yes, but only a CLASSY garbage person, like a Kardashian. Unlike a Kardashian, however, it holds a fair amount of information, and is useful to have around. I've started offering unsolicited file transfers to strangers, citing a study I made up about the dangers of cloud storage. No one's taken me up on the offer thus far, but I can tell they feel like they're declining an offer from a classy person - and that's all I can really ask for. When not in use, I leave this item on a marble end table in a way that appears artfully haphazard but actually involves meticulous planning to achieve the perfect tableau of casual sophistication. With it in my possession I stand up straighter, laugh with my head tilted back and a hand on my chest, and have the confidence to invest.

Oh, and it works well as a flash drive.

reviews.title: Tiny, but they get the job done.
reviews.text:
When I got these and finally was able to get them out of their nearly impenetrable packaging, I was surprised by how tiny they are. One is less than 1/4th the size of my actual thumb. I wasn't sure I had gotten actual flash drives, but they are and they work fine.

One reason I got these is that they are numbered 1-5 to make them easier to tell apart. However, they used Roman numerals (why?) which are just etched into the silver case, making them hard to read.

I didn't do speed tests on these because I'm not reading and writing giant files, but I ran a program from one of them with a 500MB database and everything executed instantly. No complaints at all.

reviews.title: 2G is not much these days, but these have utility for many things
reviews.text: You can’t create a bootable flash drive with less than about 8G, but for data storage these little keychain-style thumb drives are great. I am and editor/publisher whose press publishes between 12 and 15 titles a year in both Kindle and paperback formats, and I was able to save all the support files and final PDFs for the 2022 publishing year on one thumb drive. Considering how much high resolution artwork is involved, I think that’s fantastic. I can store an extra by-year backup of all our work on thumb drives like this. The five in this package will cover the last five years, and one more pack should cover most of the rest.

reviews.text: Works perfectly with my Lenovo Flex laptop and Samsung Galaxy S20 smartphone. Transfers data very fast and no hassles so far. Helpful in storing large volume files. I am still careful with this and backup valuable files just in case of loss or failure of the drive. I don't like the removable caps; I hope they'll not be lost. Also, they don’t include a little string or ring to connect to my key ring, that's a big drawback for me. The product was nicely packaged but delivered late.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: Very disappointed in the one I received.  The USB stem was inserted in the pineapple base vertically instead of horizontally, which in turn, caused the green top to be turned in the wrong direction.  It's clearly a manufacturing error...too bad the mistake passed through inspection.  I bought this flash drive specifically for the pineapple.  Mine does not look like the photo above.  Cheaply made.  I have just reordered in hopes that the next one will be correct.
